calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-16-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey Neustadt for petitioner Julalee Menke (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on petition for probate of will.

The Court noted that no new filings had been made since the last hearing. Mr. Neustadt asserted that documents had been filed and corrected, and the only item outstanding was submission of the proposed order. The Court reviewed the register of actions and found no activity since July 2025, with the last e-filing submitted in February 2025.

The Court the petition off calendar for failure to resolve procedural issues as previously directed.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jul-22-2025 Continued To Sep-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Jeffrey Neustadt, Esq., for Julalee Menke, Petitioner.

The court reminds counsel of the several outstanding issues and of the continued failure to address them. Counsel offers an update regarding the status of the estate and requests a continuance of two months to address the outstanding issues as described in the Examiner's notes. The court advises that it will grant counsel's request to continue, however, the court will take the matter off calendar if the procedural issues are not resolved by the next calling.

The matter is continued to Sep-16-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Nguyen. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Continued To Feb-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey Neustadt for Julalee Menke, Proposed Personal Representative.

The court noted that notes from the Probate Examiner were sent to counsel four months ago and had not been addressed. Mr. Neustadt claimed he had attempted to address the issues raised by the examiner, but the necessary filings were not completed due to clerical error. The court inquired about a formal response to the examiner's notes and Mr. Neustadt indicated that he would prepare a declaration for filing.

The court directed Mr. Neustadt to file the response by February 4, 2025, and continued the matter to February 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
